entities.description: A four-volume Oxford University Press edition that prints what the editors called an Earlier and a Later Version in parallel, with prologues, prefatory materials, a table of lessons, glossary, errata, and a manuscript-based critical apparatus.

reviewNotes: The cataloging boundary is one four-volume 1850 publication set, represented as one numbered item, with two version-level Middle English streams and nine U-M GROUP/TEXT streams; repeated parallel PB milestones are not additional works or physical pages.

Forshall and Madden's manuscript statements are modeled at their stated scopes. Royal MS 1 C VIII is the edition's Later Version base throughout, but current scholarship does not treat it as a transparent universal Later Version witness.
The XML retains a very large variant apparatus in NOTE1 and NOTE2 elements. The apparatus is an edition layer and is not flattened into the medieval version entities.
Confident attribution of the two versions, the General Prologue, or specific translation portions to Wyclif, Hereford, or Purvey is not accepted as established current scholarship.

The four complete Internet Archive volume PDFs, OCR files, item metadata records, and scan-data files are cached. Canonical cache files and worktree paths were verified as hard links to the same inodes.
No complete public digital facsimile was verified for any principal manuscript witness. Two sample images for Douce 369 do not constitute a complete witness facsimile and were not cached.

Access, copyright in historical wording, rights in modern encodings or control files, and provider reproduction terms are distinct assertions. Public viewing does not itself establish a reusable license.

lineage.summary: The repository artifact is the pinned 2006 University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English XML encoding of Forshall and Madden's four-volume 1850 edition. The edition prints two distinct late-fourteenth-century Middle English Bible version traditions in parallel, together with prefatory epistles, the Later Version General Prologue, additional prologues, a table of lessons, and an extensive critical apparatus. It is a composite scholarly edition made from scoped base manuscripts and wider collation, not a transcription of one manuscript and not a complete Wyclif oeuvre. This graph keeps the edition, the two version-level intellectual streams, nine encoded text streams, named manuscript witnesses, U-M text and page-image layers, four Internet Archive facsimiles, four OCR derivatives, and four control-metadata objects separate. Rights are likewise layer-specific: the published 1850 text and editorial layer are public domain in the United States and United Kingdom, while modern encoding and control-file status, provider terms, and possibly unpublished witness-specific variants require separate treatment.

catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloging unit is the exact four-volume edition-level compilation published by Josiah Forshall and Frederic Madden in 1850 and represented by University of Michigan item AFZ9170.0001.001. It coordinates the Earlier and Later Versions of the Middle English Bible in parallel, with prefatory epistles, the Later Version General Prologue, additional prologues, a table of lessons, and an extensive witness apparatus. It is one publication set, not 196 DIV1 elements, 6,405 PB occurrences, 3,314 page images, a single medieval manuscript, a single undifferentiated Bible translation, or the complete oeuvre of John Wyclif. The medieval version traditions, individual manuscript witnesses, 1850 editorial layer, U-M page-image and OCR layers, and checked-in XML remain distinct in the lineage record.

extent.description: One four-volume publication set whose XML serializes nine GROUP/TEXT streams: four Earlier Version streams and five Later Version streams. The file has 10 TEXT, 9 BODY, 1 GROUP, 196 DIV1, 2,858 DIV2, 48 DIV3, 2,942 P, no L or LG, 98,765 NOTE1, 671 NOTE2, 6,405 PB occurrences with 3,314 unique REF values, and 72,418 MILESTONE elements. The repeated PB occurrences mark positions in parallel serialization; the header's 3,314 TIFF images are the historical page-image carrier extent.

notes: The XML header's generic language value 'eng' is not used to erase the Middle English language of the medieval version text.

Royal MS 1 C VIII is the edition's continuous Later Version base but is not equated with a pure or universal Later Version: modern scholarship identifies revised Later Version strata within it.
The exact title-page phrase 'by John Wycliffe and his followers' is retained as edition history while current authorship remains conservative and scoped.

summaries.value: Forshall and Madden's 1850 four-volume scholarly edition places the Earlier and Later Versions of the Middle English Bible in parallel and preserves a large manuscript-variant apparatus. The Earlier Version is generally more literal; the Later Version revises it into more idiomatic prose while continuing engagement with the Latin Vulgate. The set also includes prefatory epistles, the anonymous General Prologue, additional prologues, and a liturgical table of lessons.

The record describes a publication-defined compilation. Neither the parallel versions nor their apparatus are silently reduced to one abstract medieval work, and no single physical manuscript is treated as the source of the entire edition.

The U-M representation is uncorrected OCR with Level-1 TEI-style encoding and linked page images. Repeated PB elements occur in parallel serialization; 6,405 PB occurrences resolve to 3,314 unique image REF values, matching the header's historical image count.
